Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Rachel Whitfield's complaint about Vectral relocation records using search_emails (query: "Rachel Whitfield" or "Vectral relocation" or similar). The agent should find Rachel's March 18 email reporting that Terrence Okafor, Camille Nguyen, and Jordan Park have records stuck at "In Progress" even though their moves were completed in early March. HR is blocking reimbursements.
OE 2: Search Slack for internal discussions about the sync/status corruption issue using conversations_search_messages (query: "sync" or "MoveSync" or "status overwrite" or "relocation status" or similar). The agent should discover two critical messages: (a) Dmitri Volkov's message in #engineering (C003) explaining that the MoveSync patch deployed March 12 only prevents future overwrites and does not retroactively fix records that were already corrupted — he also references dropping a note on "ENG-397" which was already closed; (b) Fatimah Al-Rashidi's message in #customer-engagement (C002) and #operations (C006) flagging that Mosaic Robotics records (Andre Washington / Boston and Keiko Tanaka / Houston) are also stuck at "In Progress," expanding the scope beyond Vectral.
OE 3: Search Linear for engineering tickets related to the sync issue using linear_list_issues (query: "sync" or "MoveSync" or "status overwrite" or "relocation" or similar). The agent should find ticket #397 titled "Fix MoveSync relocation status overwrite on sync runs issue" with state_id "done" on team_engineering.
OE 4: Read Linear ticket #397 comments using linear_list_comments (issue_id: "issue_5ce0435ebd2f415e9bfe9f4db4baaa73"). The closing comment says: "Patch deployed to production March 12. MoveSync job updated to skip records already in Completed status — no longer overwrites on sync runs. Standard test suite passing. Closing this ticket." This confirms the fix but does not mention retroactive correction of already-corrupted records.
OE 5: Search Airtable for all relocation records with incorrect "In Progress" status using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (table: tblRelocations01, filter by Status: "In Progress" or search broadly). The agent should find 5 affected records across 2 clients: Terrence Okafor (Vectral, Chicago, completed March 8), Camille Nguyen (Vectral, Seattle, completed March 7), Jordan Park (Vectral, Austin, completed March 9), Andre Washington (Mosaic Robotics, Boston, completed March 6), and Keiko Tanaka (Mosaic Robotics, Houston, completed March 10). The agent must search broadly — filtering only by "Vectral" would miss the 2 Mosaic records.
OE 6: Update all 5 Airtable records from "In Progress" to "Completed" using airtable_update_records (record_ids: rece48bdecea1af4e, rec6ff7c1db59d44d, receee354b28a5d43, rec18494bda03df49, rec8086a371fb7a48; field: Status, value: "Completed").
OE 7: Create a new Linear ticket (via linear_create_issue) or reopen/update ticket #397 (via linear_update_issue) to track the retroactive backfill remediation that was not addressed by the March 12 patch. The ticket should describe the need to correct records corrupted before the patch date and should be assigned to someone on Samira Tariq's engineering team.
OE 8: Search QuickBooks for invoices tied to these completed relocations using quickbooks_search_invoices (query: "Vectral" or "Mosaic" or "INV-3042" or "INV-3047" or similar). The agent should find INV-3042 ($3,200 for Terrence Okafor / Vectral Systems) and INV-3047 ($4,100 for Andre Washington / Mosaic Robotics) to verify that billing amounts are consistent with the corrected records.
OE 9: Look up contact details for Rachel Whitfield, Tamara Byrd, Samira Tariq, and others using contacts_search_contacts or contacts_get_contacts.
OE 10: Reply to Rachel Whitfield's email or send a new email to rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com (via reply_to_email or send_email) confirming that the relocation records for Terrence Okafor, Camille Nguyen, and Jordan Park have been corrected to "Completed" and that expense reimbursements should no longer be blocked.
OE 11: Send email to tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com (via send_email) notifying her that Mosaic Robotics relocation records for Andre Washington and Keiko Tanaka were also affected by a system sync issue and have been corrected.
OE 12: Send a message to Samira Tariq (via send_email to samira.tariq@moveops.com or via conversations_add_message in #engineering or DM) informing her that the MoveSync patch deployed March 12 did not retroactively fix corrupted records, and that her team needs to complete the backfill work or verify the manual corrections are sufficient.
OE 13: Log a CRM engagement on the Vectral Systems account (via crm_create_engagement) documenting the sync issue, the 3 affected employees, and the resolution, so account manager Mina Hashimoto is informed.
OE 14: Log a CRM engagement on the Mosaic Robotics account (via crm_create_engagement) documenting the sync issue, the 2 affected employees, and the resolution, so account manager Emeka Diallo is informed.
OE 15: Create a calendar event on April 29, 2026 (via calendar_add_calendar_event) for Chloe Vance to sync with engineering about the MoveSync backfill completion.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2The agent called linear_create_issue with team 'Engineering' and the tool returned successfully (output_file tool_call_result_17.json). A new ticket was created to track the retroactive backfill work.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows a linear_update_issue call that sets the issue (issue_5ce0435ebd2f415e9bfe9f4db4baaa73, ENG-397) state to 'In Progress', effectively reopening it. The tool result confirms the state_id changed to 'In Progress'. A detailed comment was also added via linear_create_comment.
✓Run #4The agent called linear_update_issue on issue_5ce0435ebd2f415e9bfe9f4db4baaa73 (ENG-397), setting state to 'In Progress' with priority 1 (Urgent), and the tool returned success. A detailed comment was also added via linear_create_comment.
✓Run #5The agent called linear_update_issue with id 'issue_5ce0435ebd2f415e9bfe9f4db4baaa73', setting state to 'In Progress' (reopening it) and priority 1 (Urgent). The tool returned a result file indicating success.
✓Run #6The agent called linear_update_issue on issue_5ce0435ebd2f415e9bfe9f4db4baaa73 (Linear #397), changing its state to 'In Progress'. The tool result confirms success.
5 The Linear ticket title or description mentions the MoveSync backfill, retroactive correction of corrupted relocation records, or data remediation for records affected before the March 12 patch (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The linear_update_issue description explicitly references 'DATA REMEDIATION', lists all 5 corrupted records, and states the patch did not retroactively fix them. The comment also calls for a 'comprehensive sweep' and backfill verification before closing.
✓Run #2The Linear ticket title is 'Data remediation: Backfill corrupted relocation statuses from MoveSync bug (ENG-397 follow-up)' and the description explicitly references that the 'patch did not retroactively fix records that were already corrupted before the patch date' and lists required actions including a full audit.
✓Run #3The linear_create_comment body explicitly mentions 'backfill', 'corrupted before the patch date', the 5 affected records, and states 'This ticket should not be closed again until engineering confirms no additional corrupted records exist and the backfill is complete.' The update reopened the ticket for this purpose.
✓Run #4The linear_create_comment body explicitly describes the 'corrupted data was never backfilled after patch deployment,' lists all 5 affected records requiring retroactive correction, and specifies the backfill/audit as the action needed before the ticket can be closed.
✓Run #5The linear_update_issue description explicitly states 'The March 12 patch only prevents future overwrites. It does NOT retroactively fix records corrupted before the patch date...Manual backfill of all 5 records completed April 26. Engineering must: (1) Confirm no additional corrupted records exist beyond the 5 identified.' This clearly references the retroactive backfill requirement.
✓Run #6The linear_update_issue description explicitly includes a 'DATA REMEDIATION' section detailing the backfill of corrupted records, naming all 5 affected employees, and specifying closure criteria requiring data remediation completion.

outcome

16 The final response identifies the root cause as the MoveSync sync job overwriting relocation statuses (or describes a sync process that reverted completed records to incorrect statuses).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The final response explicitly identifies the MoveSync sync job as the root cause, stating it 'was overwriting Completed relocation statuses back to In Progress during routine sync runs' due to missing logic to skip terminal-state records.
✓Run #2The final response clearly states 'The MoveSync sync job (tracked in Linear as ENG-397) was overwriting relocation statuses during routine sync runs, reverting "Completed" records back to "In Progress."'
✓Run #3The final response clearly identifies the root cause: 'A bug in the MoveSync synchronization tool (tracked as ENG-397 in Linear) was overwriting relocation statuses during routine sync runs. Completed relocations were being reverted to "In Progress."'
✓Run #4The final response states: 'MoveSync sync job bug (ENG-397) was overwriting relocation status values during routine sync runs, reverting Completed records back to In Progress.' This clearly identifies the root cause.
✓Run #5The final response clearly states: 'The MoveSync sync job (tracked as ENG-397) was overwriting relocation statuses during routine data synchronization, reverting completed moves back to In Progress.'
✓Run #6The final response explicitly identifies the root cause as 'The MoveSync synchronization job had a bug that overwrote relocation statuses during sync runs, reverting completed records back to In Progress.'

outcome

✓Run #3The final response explicitly states 'Engineering deployed a patch on March 12 to skip records in terminal states — but nobody ran a backfill on records already corrupted before the patch date.' This directly addresses the forward-looking-only nature of the patch.
✓Run #4The final response states: 'Engineering deployed a patch on March 12 that prevents future overwrites — but nobody ran a backfill on the already-corrupted records.' This explicitly identifies the forward-only nature of the patch.

This conveys that the patch did not retroactively fix the already-corrupted records. The Slack message to Samira further explicitly states 'the patch only fixed the forward-looking behavior.'

19 The final response addresses the invoicing status for the affected relocations, identifying invoices INV-3042 ($3,200 for Terrence Okafor / Vectral) and/or INV-3047 ($4,100 for Andre Washington / Mosaic), or notes that not all affected moves have corresponding invoices.
outcome
 ✓✗✓✗✓✓ 67%

✗Run #2The final response only states 'All invoices tied to these moves are accurate — no billing discrepancies found' without identifying specific invoice numbers (INV-3042 or INV-3047) and without noting that only 2 of the 5 affected moves have corresponding invoices. The QuickBooks search retrieved invoices but none matching the specific affected employees were surfaced in the response.
✓Run #3The final response explicitly references 'INV-3042 (Terrence Okafor, $3,200) and INV-3047 (Andre Washington, $4,100) confirmed accurate. No invoicing discrepancies found for the other 3 records.' This identifies both invoices and notes the absence of invoices for the other 3 moves.
✗Run #4The agent ran QuickBooks searches but did not surface or identify INV-3042 ($3,200 for Okafor/Vectral) or INV-3047 ($4,100 for Washington/Mosaic). The final response only says 'No billing discrepancies found' without identifying the specific invoices or noting that only 2 of 5 affected moves have invoices. The specific invoice numbers expected by the criterion were not found or reported.
✓Run #5The final response explicitly states: 'Invoices verified — INV-3042 (Okafor, $3,200) and INV-3047 (Washington, $4,100) are accurate, no discrepancies.' Both invoice numbers and amounts are called out.

query construction
